title: 1962, 1965, 1971: Army veteran who fought 3 wars now in '4th battle' to save his land
content: A 92-year-old retired Army Captain lost his land to fraud. He fought in three wars for the nation's defense. His land was fraudulently registered and transferred without his knowledge. An FIR was registered after intervention by the Superintendent of Police. An investigation is now underway to reclaim his property.

title: PM Modi, his New Zealand counterpart Luxon announce Annual Maritime Security Dialogue
content: India and New Zealand established a strategic partnership roadmap for enhanced cooperation. Both nations will maintain regular defense ministry and service-level engagements. They will cooperate under Combined Task Force 150 in the Middle East. Maritime cooperation will be strengthened through new arrangements and naval exercises. An annual Maritime Security Dialogue will also be established.

title: India's 1,601-day reign as World No. 1 ends after England complete 4-0 T20I whitewash
content: England completed a 4-0 T20I series sweep over India with a 56-run win in Southampton. Jos Buttler smashed a career-best 131, while Harry Brook's unbeaten 95 helped England post 257/3 with a record 233-run stand. India replied with 201/8 despite half-centuries from Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma, ending a disappointing tour on a losing note.

title: Trump threatens Iran with '1000 missiles' as both sides shoot their mouth in Gulf spat
content: President Trump threatened Iran with massive missile strikes if assassination plots are pursued. This warning dramatically escalated tensions between the two nations. Disagreements over a maritime deal's interpretation fueled renewed military exchanges. Iran vowed revenge for past actions and perceived US treaty violations. Ambiguity and mistrust now threaten another fragile understanding between them.

title: Hours after Zuckerberg posted about AI model on Musk’s X, he gets business proposal
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the new Muse Spark 1.1 AI model on X. X's Head of Product Nikita Bier then offered Creator Monetization for his account. This exchange occurred on Elon Musk's platform after Zuckerberg's AI announcement. The Muse Spark 1.1 model is designed for coding and complex AI tasks. Developers can now access this powerful new AI through Meta's API.

title: Wangchuk refuses to end fast, Omar targets Centre over Ladakh-J&K ‘double standards’
content: Leh Apex Body postponed a Delhi trip as Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike. J&K CM Omar Abdullah criticized the Centre's double standards regarding Ladakh and J&K. The Centre agreed to explore Article 371-like safeguards for Ladakh's elected body. Abdullah questioned why Ladakh receives constitutional protections while J&K statehood is denied. He also alleged a BJP offer to an NC MLA for switching sides.

title: J&K: Interpol notice against Hizbul terrorist wanted in cop killings
content: An Interpol Red Corner Notice has been issued for Hizbul Mujahideen terrorist Imtiyaz Ahmad Kandoo. This notice targets Kandoo for his alleged involvement in a deadly 2013 attack. The attack resulted in the deaths of four police officers in Sopore, Kashmir. Kandoo, a designated terrorist, is believed to have exfiltrated to Pakistan. The State Investigation Agency considers this a significant achievement in pursuing fugitives.

title: Personnel dept cancels FCI ED suspension, terms food ministry order legally non-existent
content: The personnel department cancelled a food ministry order suspending an FCI executive director. This suspension was treated as legally non-existent from the beginning by the DoPT. The officer was ordered for immediate repatriation to his parent cadre by the DoPT. The food ministry had initiated proceedings after a committee found alleged irregularities in rice sales. A review committee was also set up to improve administrative efficiency within the FCI.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Do you have a 'phone body'? 6 subtle signs your phone has changed you
content: Modern device use impacts posture and vision significantly.  Repeated neck strain can lead to discomfort and altered body appearance.  Smartwatch wear may cause skin irritation and sensitivities for some users.  Grip strength declines, indicating reduced physical activity and different body usage.  These physical changes are reversible with healthier habits and more real-world engagement.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai on the one AI race Google is ‘losing’ to Anthropic and OpenAI
content: Google's CEO acknowledges falling behind rivals in agentic coding technology.  This crucial AI capability has become the industry's most lucrative battleground.  Google's new tools, Antigravity 2.0 and Gemini 3.5 Flash, aim to close this gap.  Internal adoption shows promise, but external usage lags behind competitors.  Google is also adjusting pricing to attract heavy coding workloads.

title: 'Be your own hero': Sonam Wangchuk rejects 'modern Gandhi' tag amid hunger strike
content: Educator Sonam Wangchuk rejects hero labels and urges citizens to take responsibility. He is currently on an indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar for 14 days. Wangchuk is protesting examination irregularities and seeking accountability from the government. He has lost significant weight and feels tired but remains determined. Wangchuk appeals for public support in the ongoing movement and upcoming march.

title: Watch: Gang uses Thar to uproot ATM, escapes with cash in Odisha
content: Thieves uprooted an ATM in Balasore and dragged it away using a Mahindra Thar. The incident occurred early Saturday morning under Khaira police station limits. CCTV footage captured the entire sequence of events as it unfolded. The accused broke into the kiosk and fled with the cash inside. Police are now investigating and examining the footage to identify the suspects.

title: Who are Lamine Yamal's parents? Meet Mounir Nasraoui and Sheila Ebana
content: Lamine Yamal's parents, Mounir Nasraoui from Morocco and Sheila Ebana from Equatorial Guinea, both immigrated to Spain and raised Yamal in the greater Barcelona area under modest financial conditions. After separating when Yamal was around three years old, his mother raised him in Rocafonda, Mataró, while working to make ends meet. His name honors two men who helped his family during a period of hardship. Despite his Spanish nationality and international football career with La Roja, Yamal proudly represents his Moroccan and Equatorial Guinean heritage, wearing his parents' national flags on his boots at every match.

title: NHL trade rumors: Canucks eye Maple Leafs' $46.5m winger
content: Matthew Knies trade speculation continues to build around the Toronto Maple Leafs, with the Vancouver Canucks emerging as a team being actively encouraged to pursue the 23-year-old winger. Knies, coming off a 66-point season and carrying a $7. 75M cap hit through 2031, holds no trade protection in his contract. While Leafs management views him as core to their future, rival teams keep calling. Any realistic offer would need to include NHL-ready defensive pieces, premium draft picks, and a top prospect to get Toronto's attention.

title: NBA trade rumors: Lakers emerge as favorites for Hawks' $5m forward
content: The Los Angeles Lakers are reportedly in discussions with the Atlanta Hawks about a sign-and-trade for Jonathan Kuminga, who is said to prefer a move to Los Angeles. While negotiations remain ongoing, the versatile forward is viewed as a strong fit for the Lakers' long-term plans. If completed, the deal could become one of the NBA offseason's biggest blockbuster moves.

title: Govinda says he was sent videos of wife Sunita abusing on Lock Upp
content: Facing health challenges, Sunita Ahuja made the decision to leave Lock Upp season two. Govinda, her husband, along with Tina, paid her a visit on the show. During this time, Govinda shed light on Sunita's hardships, noting her use of strong language inside the house. Sunita has openly discussed her feelings of infidelity in her marriage, stating that her children are her primary focus despite the difficulties.

title: Historic! Noskova becomes 3rd Czech woman in 4 years to win Wimbledon
content: Linda Noskova won her first Grand Slam title, beating fellow Czech Karolina Muchova 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 in the Wimbledon final. The 21-year-old recovered after squandering five match points in the second set to seal victory on her sixth chance. The triumph makes her the youngest Wimbledon champion since Petra Kvitova in 2011 and lifts her to a career-high No. 7.

title: J&K: BSF woman constable dies by suicide in Jammu
content: A 31-year-old BSF constable allegedly died by suicide in Jammu's Gadigarh camp. Constable Vanita Rani was found hanging from a ceiling fan with a dupatta. Her body was sent for a postmortem examination to establish the cause of death. This incident follows another woman constable's alleged suicide in Jammu on June 29. Both incidents are currently under investigation by authorities.

title: R Madhavan earned Rs 70,000-80,000 in Mumbai, thought his first acting job was a scam
content: Actor R Madhavan wrote his ambition to become rich and famous as a teenager. He believed in dreaming big and never limiting one's aspirations. Madhavan was teaching communication skills before acting came into his life. An unexpected street encounter led him to pursue a career in cinema. This chance meeting resulted in his debut in the successful film Alaipayuthey.

title: Hyderabad student found dead in Finland two months after going missing
content: An 18-year-old Hyderabad student's body was recovered from the sea in Helsinki. Finnish authorities confirmed the recovery of Manideep Reddy's body after two months. His family has raised serious questions about the circumstances surrounding his death. They believe the case has not been properly investigated by Finnish authorities. The family urges the Indian government to facilitate their travel to Finland.

title: Ram Kapoor recalls being molested at 13 in boarding school
content: During his appearance on Lock Upp Season 2, actor Ram Kapoor courageously opened up about a childhood molestation incident that profoundly affected his life. He shared how this traumatic experience shaped his relationships, revealing that only his wife Gautami knew of this secret. Ram expressed gratitude for his journey toward healing and acceptance, highlighting the importance of speaking out against such abuses.

title: Rajinikanth, CM Vijay, Kamal Haasan and others pour tributes as S Janaki passes away
content: Legendary playback singer S Janaki passed away at the age of 88. Her extensive career spanned over 48,000 songs in many languages. Tributes poured in from across the Indian film industry. Rajinikanth and Kamal Haasan shared heartfelt messages remembering her voice. Her indelible mark on music will be cherished by generations.

title: 'Slapped, dragged by hair': UP woman alleges assault; video shows attack as cop watches
content: A woman faced assault and threats during a road rage incident in Gorakhpur. Her vehicle had a minor collision with a motorcycle, leading to an argument. A group of men then attacked her violently in the presence of police. One accused has been arrested, and a search is on for others. Police have registered an FIR and invoked serious charges against the suspects.

title: Harry Brook creates history in 5th T20I against India, becomes 1st England batter to...
content: England captain Harry Brook achieved a significant milestone in the final T20I. He became the first English batter to surpass two hundred runs in a home bilateral series. Brook also set a new record for the most runs against India in a bilateral T20I series. His performance eclipsed Nicholas Pooran's previous record of one hundred eighty-four runs. This series cemented Brook's dominance against the Indian team.

title: 'Poisoned soft drink': Man kills son, dies by suicide over wife's extended stay at parents' home
content: A man allegedly poisoned his four-year-old son and himself after a dispute with his wife. The wife had refused to leave her parental home following her sister's wedding ceremony. The child died en route to the hospital after his condition deteriorated rapidly. The father later consumed poison and died during treatment at the hospital. Police are investigating the exact circumstances surrounding this tragic incident.

title: IND vs ENG: Why is Sooryavanshi not playing the series finale?
content: India dropped teenage opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for Sanju Samson in the fifth T20I against England, while Suryansh Shedge replaced Washington Sundar. Explaining the changes, captain Shreyas Iyer said the team wanted to "try what's the best going forward for us as a team" and urged players to give "hundred percent effort, irrespective of the result". England then piled up 257/3, powered by Jos Buttler's 131 and Harry Brook's unbeaten 95.

title: 'Heavy winds and high waves': Vietnam boat tragedy kills 15 Indians - what we know so far
content: Tragedy struck as a speedboat transporting fifteen Indian tourists capsized near Phu Quoc island in Vietnam, claiming their lives. The accident occurred while the vessel was making its return from Hon May Rut Island to An Thoi Port, where thirty-six passengers were onboard. Rescue operations faced challenges due to rough sea conditions and individuals trapped within the vessel.

title: 'Stabbed 10 times': Nursing student killed after blocking stalker's calls
content: A 24-year-old man was arrested for fatally stabbing a 19-year-old pharmacy student. The victim had rejected his advances and blocked his phone number previously. He confronted her in her rented room and attacked her with a knife. The accused had also threatened her brother and her café employer. Police arrested the man after he fled towards Raipur following the incident.

title: Pakistan turns to army chief Asim Munir in battle against population growth
content: Asim Munir, the chief of the Pakistani army, has taken a notable role in a prominent committee tasked with tackling the pressing issue of population growth in the country. The committee aims to devise strategies to manage the rapidly increasing numbers. Federal Health Minister Syed Mustafa Kamal emphasized the critical nature of this initiative, in addition to talks on revising the National Finance Commission Award system.

title: India tops China on new millionaires, keeps money the old way
content: India added over 31,000 new dollar millionaires in 2025, significantly outpacing China.  Indian wealthy individuals hold most assets in real estate and gold, unlike Western economies.  This preference for tangible assets contrasts with a greater financialization seen elsewhere.  While wealth is growing, it remains concentrated in a few major cities.

title: Mom lying dead, son rushes for help: Indian-origin techie killed by husband in Georgia
content: In a shocking domestic violence incident in Georgia, a man is charged after allegedly shooting his son and killing his wife. The injured son sought refuge with neighbors while Sheetal Wrzesien, the victim, a tech leader, was lying dead inside the house. The husband was arrested and charged with felony murder.

title: 15 Indians killed in Vietnam tragedy: 10 from TN, 3 from Andhra - embassy releases list
content: Fifteen Indian nationals perished in a tragic boat accident off Vietnam's coast on Saturday. The speedboat carrying thirty-six people capsized near May Rut Ngoai Islet, prompting a swift rescue effort. Tourist boats arrived quickly, but some passengers remained trapped inside the overturned vessel. The Indian embassy released the names of the deceased, with most hailing from Tamil Nadu.

title: Legendary singer S Janaki passes away at 88 in Mysuru due to age-related ailments
content: Veteran playback singer S Janaki, known as Nightingale of South India, passed away at 88. She died at Apollo Hospital in Mysuru after battling age-related health issues. Janaki Amma had declined the Padma Bhushan award in 2013, feeling it came too late. She received four National Film Awards and thirty-three State Film Awards during her career. The legendary singer had lent her voice to over twenty Indian languages.

title: As India remain stuck in traffic, England players enjoy football - Watch
content: Ahead of the fifth T20I in Southampton, England players played a game of rondo football after India's arrival was delayed due to traffic. The delay pushed the toss to 7:15 PM IST and the start to 7:30 PM IST. Once India reached the venue, Shreyas Iyer and Suryansh Shedge began warm-ups as England chased a 4-0 series win.

title: 7/12, Khata, Jamabandi or Patta? A guide to land ownership documents across India
content: Land records are crucial for various property transactions across India. Different states use unique names like 7/12 extract and Khatauni for these documents. These records detail ownership, cultivation, and tax liabilities for land parcels. While terminology differs, their core purpose remains consistent for all citizens. Digitization efforts are making this vital information increasingly accessible online.

title: 'Operation Lotus' in J&K? CM Omar Abdullah claims BJP offered MLAs Rs 20-30 crore
content: Jammu and Kashmir's Chief Minister Omar Abdullah condemned the Bharatiya Janata Party for what he called attempts to destabilize his administration. He revealed that BJP legislators were allegedly offered lucrative financial rewards and government positions to switch loyalties. Abdullah claimed the BJP's promises of statehood to National Conference MLAs were a tactic to sway them.

title: Jayden Adams cause of death: South Africa mourns World Cup midfielder
content: South Africa midfielder Jayden Adams has died at 25, just weeks after featuring at the FIFA World Cup 2026. His cause of death has not been disclosed. The Mamelodi Sundowns star earned widespread tributes from football officials, teammates, and fans, who remembered his talent, commitment, and bright future after representing Bafana Bafana on the world stage.

title: Kerala toddler dies after anaesthesia for lip cut; doctor booked for negligence
content: An anaesthetist faces negligence charges after a toddler died following anaesthesia administration. The one-and-a-half-year-old boy received anaesthesia for a lip injury treatment. His condition worsened, and he was moved to another hospital for further care. The child unfortunately passed away while undergoing intensive treatment in the ICU. Hospital authorities have denied the allegations and stated all possible treatment was provided.

title: Rs 17L looted in 28 seconds: CCTV captures gunpoint robbery by bike-borne men in Delhi
content: An employee was robbed of seventeen lakh rupees in east Delhi's Gandhi Nagar market. Two bike-borne assailants threatened him at gunpoint and snatched his cash bag. CCTV footage shows the incident occurring around 4:09 pm on Friday afternoon. Police are analyzing the video to identify and apprehend the two accused individuals. The preliminary investigation suggests the robbers had been following the victim.

title: Raja blasts PCB over Masood sacking: 'When a captain is given ... '
content: Former Pakistan captain Ramiz Raja criticised the PCB for removing Shan Masood as Test captain and reinstating Babar Azam. Defending Shan, Raja said the skipper was unfairly blamed despite having an "ordinary" squad, adding, “If the standard of the Test squad is not good, how is the captain only responsible for this.” Pakistan next tour the West Indies and England.

title: Families of Vanniyar reservation martyrs also deserve State government jobs, says Anbumani
content: While welcoming the compassion shown by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay to the kin of the Karur stampede victims, he asserts that such relief must not be limited to one group alone

title: The nightingale who stole Kerala’s heart
content: Janaki’s effortless singing and pleasant nature earned her enduring love and admiration across generations

title: VCK turns its focus to Tamil nationalism post-election
content: VCK founder and Chidambaram MP Thol. Thirumavalavan has urged the party to gear up for a ‘Tamil nationalist’ public rally on August 17 in Ulundurpet near Villupuram

title: When Bollywood jived to S. Janaki’s infectious voice
content: In the 1980s, when there was a massive influx of South Indian production houses into Bollywood, remaking South Indian hits into Hindi, Janaki emerged as a pan-Indian voice

title: S. Janaki: Not Kerala-born, but forever its voice
content: With her passing, Kerala mourns not merely a legendary playback singer from Andhra Pradesh, but an artiste whose songs dissolved linguistic boundaries and became woven into the emotional fabric of the State’s cultural life.

title: V.D. Satheesan condoles S. Janaki’s demise, says she was ‘the voice that captivated generations’
content: Kerala Chief Minister says her contributions to Indian film music transcended the title ‘the nightingale of south India’

title: A lullaby falls silent: Kannada cinema bids farewell to S. Janaki
content: Among the tributes that poured in from the Kannada music and film fraternities, one sentiment stood out: her extraordinary versatility

title: Four of family found dead at house in Bengaluru’s Kottigepalya
content: Police said their preliminary investigation suggests that Prashanth is suspected to have fatally attacked the three family members before his death.

title: Amit Shah assures church leaders that FCRA Bill will not be applied retrospectively
content: In the meeting held with Mr. Shah, the Minister asked the church leaders to file FIRs in all cases of aggression against the community, urges them to broker peace in Manipur and also reassured them that the Bill was not against any religious community

title: POCSO case registered, after 14-year-old girl gives birth in Hubballi
content: A case under Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012,has been registered in Hubballi after a case of a fourteen year old girl giving birth came to light

title: Mediation helps 15 couple to reunite burying differences
content: Fifteen couples reunited burying their differences during the mediation proceedings at Family court and subsequently exchanged garlands to mark their reconciliation in Dharwad

title: Government intervenes as shipping shocks expose container vulnerability
content: The first outcome of the ₹10,000 crore container manufacturing scheme announced in Union Budget 2026-27 came on July 3, when an India-made EXIM container built by DCM Shriram Group was unveiled at Dadri for Maersk, which has placed a follow-on order for another 1,000 containers

title: S. Janaki’s fresh voice, improvisation made Kerala groove to her tunes
content: Janaki inimitably lent her voice to numerous evergreen Malayalam film songs from the 1960s to the 1990s. Songs such as Manjanikombil, Thenum Vayambum, Nadha Nee varum, Manassiluranroo, Akale Akale Neelakasham, Ilam Manjin have retained their freshness and ability to influence the listener.

title: Three more held in Bengal lynching case
content: A mob had lynched the man on suspicion of the rape and murder of a 11-year-old girl in Baruipur; five arrested so far in the case

title: Bengal CM meets family of murdered girl, offers compensation to youth’s family in Baruipur
content: The Chief Minister blamed illegal liquor vends and drug-related activities for contributing to the volatile situation and announced for a Statewide crackdown

title: Monsoon tracker updates: Heavy to very heavy rain lashes parts of India
content: In Uttarakhand, heavy rainfall triggered landslides, blocking 120 roads, while around 100 pilgrims were escorted to safety across a washed-out stretch of the Yamunotri National Highway after the route remained cut off for two days

title: State government will release policy to set up ‘innovation city’, says Aadhav Arjuna
content: In his address at the 35th convocation of Sathyabama Institute of Science and Technology, he says facilities must be made available for the students who like to invent, innovate, and try out their ideas

title: One-fourth of India’s monsoon rain evaporates mid-air, says new study
content: Researchers at IITM Pune say 25% of the rain mass evaporates during June to September; this may vary across regions; the finding will help scientists refine weather and climate models and better understand the monsoon

title: NIA chargesheets eight, including Pakistan-based  ‘terrorist’, in Ambala car blast case
content: Case relates to a car bomb blast in the parking area of a police station in Haryana in January; NIA alleges that Shehzad Bhatti had recruited and radicalised several through social media platforms and encrypted communication channels

title: Kerala remembers the divine voice of S. Janaki
content: Composers, lyricists and singers remember the legendary singer’s enchanting voice, pitch-perfect tonality and enduring influence

title: Naidu, Pawan Kalyan, Durgesh mourn S. Janaki’s demise
content: Chief Minister says the ‘Nightingale of South India’ built an unparalleled musical legacy spanning over six decades; Deputy Chief Minister and Cinematography Minister describe her death as an irreparable loss to Indian music.

title: Red Corner Notice issued against Hizbul terrorist hiding in Pakistan: J&K Police
content: Hizbul Mujahideen terrorist Imtiyaz Ahmad Kandoo alias Fayaz alias Sajad, who is the accused in the 2013 Hygam terror attack in Sopore that claimed the lives of four J&K Police personnel, is believed to have exfiltrated to Pakistan

title: Congress steps up Ram Temple campaign, asks Modi to answer for alleged donation theft
content: Congress party holds 26 press conferences across the country, holds Prime Minister Narendra Modi “directly answerable to the nation” as he had taken political ownership of the temple project

title: Almost 6.4% of voter names deleted in draft lists published for four States and one U.T. undergoing SIR
content: Almost 30% of voter names were deleted in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu, largely because BLOs failed to trace these voters; Odisha had a 6% deletion rate, mostly due to migration

title: Pallapatla mourns its illustrious daughter S. Janaki
content: Residents recall the legendary singer’s childhood in the village as tributes pour in, with Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu saying her timeless voice touched generations

title: Odisha govt. orders crime branch probe into errors in school textbooks
content: Earlier, four senior officers were suspended; the State government had acknowledged 1,678 errors in textbooks

title: Manipur clash bid thwarted by security forces: Police
content: A mob, allegedly from the Kuki-dominated Kangpokpi district, advanced towards a village in the Meitei-majority Imphal West district, officials say

title: West Bengal forms committee to review draft UCC Bill
content: A notification issued by the State government says the committee is formed in view of “wide ramifications and voluminous nature of the subject domain of the proposed legislation”

title: Repair work at water treatment plant disrupts drinking water supply in Shivamogga
content: Officers of Shivamogga City Corporation and the Karnataka Urban Water Supply and Drainage Board issued a statement that there was a leakage in the clariflocculator that plays a major role in water treatment.

title: Sporadic COVID-19 cases are here to stay, says official after two deaths
content: State steps up surveillance and hospital preparedness after two COVID-19 deaths in 10 days, while health authorities urge the public not to panic and await genome sequencing reports

title: Three Andhra Pradesh tourists among 15 Indians killed in Vietnam boat tragedy
content: Two persons from Kadapa and one from Machilipatnam are among the victims; Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu instructs officials to provide all possible assistance to the bereaved families and ensure the safe return of the survivors

title: Watch: ‘Nightingale of South India’ S. Janaki passes away at 88
content: Legendary playback singer S. Janaki has passed away at the age of 88 in Mysuru after battling age-related ailments. Fondly known as the “Nightingale of South India”, Janaki recorded over 20,000 songs across Tamil, Telugu, Malayalam, Kannada and Hindi during a remarkable career spanning more than six decades.

title: Youths allegedly tonsured by mob in Perumbavoor accuse police of bias
content: The trio were allegedly assaulted, stripped, publicly humiliated, and forcibly tonsured by a mob, which accused them of supplying ganja to a schoolboy

title: Vietnam boat tragedy: Kottarakkara couple among 15 Indian tourists dead
content: A.C. Thomas, 57, and his wife Loveny Thomas, 56, both residents of Kottarakkara, had left for Vietnam on July 7 as part of a group tour

title: Embezzlement at Ram Temple is a disgrace, ‘we feel humiliated’, says Nripendra Misra
content: ‘This matter now concerns the management of the temple, and it is certain that improvements will be made to prevent a recurrence of this alleged donation theft,’ the temple construction committee’s chairperson, among the first to call for the appointment of a CEO, says

title: Vande Mataram first, Jana Gana Mana second, reiterates Centre
content: Govt. issues directive to States and Central Ministries again; seeks strict compliance; Parliament to soon table Bill that makes any obstruction to the singing of Vande Mataram a punishable offence

title: The recluse next door: How Shabad woke to unimaginable horror
content: Flashing police lights replaced the stillness of two farming villages in Shabad before dawn, as residents woke up to sealed homes, grieving families and questions over whether prompt police action could have prevented the killings

title: Samji Arattupuzha’s new song captures the nostalgia of Kerala church band music
content: Why Samji Arattupuzha’s new song feels like Christmas at a Kerala Church. He has over 500 Christian devotional songs to his credit

title: LDF councillors to stage dharna against ‘drastic drop’ in plan allocation in revised State Budget
content: From ₹133.34 crore in the last Budget of the previous LDF government, the allocation has plummeted to ₹23.67 crore in the UDF government’s revised Budget

title: Role of AI is only that of an assistant, not a judge, says Chief Justice of India
content: Whether digital dispute resolution and AI enhance or disrupt access to justice depends on how thoughtfully and deliberately their use is designed, says Chief Justice Kant

title: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Updates: "Boat Had Barely Begun Moving, Tipped Over Before We Could React": Survivor
content: Indian Tourists Killed in Vietnam Boat Incident News Updates: At least 15 Indians, including 13 men and 2 women, were killed in the incident. There were a total of 36 people on board the vessel.

title: Iran Officials Admit To "Mistake" After Attacks On Ships In Hormuz: Report
content: According to the officials, Tehran blamed the incidents on an "errant" faction of hardliners that allegedly acted to derail ongoing negotiations.

title: Man Arrested For Raping 12-Year-Old Neighbour In Assam's Sivasagar
content: The accused, a fast-food vendor from Samastipur district of Bihar, lived in a rented house in the same neighbourhood as the survivor.

title: 2 Arrested After Houses Set On Fire In Manipur, Security Forces Avert Clash
content: The Manipur Police said the two arrested people have been identified as Kammang Lhouvum, 65, and Pagin Hangshing, 30

title: 16-Year-Old Electrocuted In Karnataka, Parents File Case Against Power Board
content: A 16-year-old boy allegedly died of electrocution after coming into contact with a low-hanging electric wire connected to a government school in Karnataka's Tumakuru district.

title: Bengaluru Woman Says Delivery Agent Barged Into Home To Use Toilet, Flashed Her
content: She said the delivery agent had come to her house to deliver a parcel and asked her for permission to use the washroom. When she refused, he forcibly entered the house.

title: 5 Killed Inside Well Due To Toxic Fumes From Generator In Shillong
content: Four workers and a local resident were killed due to toxic fumes from a generator while working inside a well

title: Boat Travelled Barely 300 Metres Before Toppling: Indian Witness In Vietnam
content: A boat carrying a group of Indian tourists that capsized off an island in south Vietnam on Saturday had barely travelled 300-400 metres from the shore when it suddenly overturned, an Indian national who witnessed the tragedy said.

title: 'Compromised Leaders": Congress Sees Infighting Over Replacing Punjab Chief
content: On July 1, the Congress announced that Amrinder Singh RajaWarring would continue as the Punjab unit president and appointed Charanjit Singh Channi as chairperson of the campaign committee.

title: 15 Indian Tourists Killed As Boat Overturns In Vietnam
content: Search and rescue operations are underway, and the Indian mission has set up control rooms to assist those affected.

title: Some Jumped Out, I Saw Him Get Stuck: Friend Of Man Killed In Vietnam Tragedy
content: Nirmal Kumar, one of the survivors, said the vessel was a closed speedboat carrying 32 passengers and that it overturned within three minutes of setting off.

title: Woman Filed Petition For Cheaper Cancer Drug. She Died Waiting For Verdict
content: The patient had pleaded with the court in 2022 to lower the cost of astronomically priced cancer medicines.

title: Pune Building Collapse Death Count Rises To 8, One Still Missing In Debris
content: The death count in the Moshi waste processing unit collapse near Pune has risen to eight while 14 of the estimated 23 persons stuck inside have been rescued, a senior official said on Saturday evening.

title: Scare Force One? Report Flags Security Gaps In Trump's New Plane
content: The Qatari jet is acting as a stopgap until two brand new Boeing Air Force Ones are delivered later this decade following a series of delays. After that, it is due to be donated to Trump's planned presidential library.

title: Lion Snatches Boy, 11, From Hill In Gujarat, Mauls Him To Death In Forest
content: The incident took place around 5:45 am near the 50th step of the pilgrimage route. The family, from Modaj village in Kheda district, was climbing the hill when the lion suddenly attacked in the presence of hundreds of devotees.

title: "A Good Indian Is A Dead Indian": Truck Drivers Reveal Abuse In Australia
content: While an Indian-origin truck driver was speaking in Punjabi at a truck stop in South Australia, someone spat on him and disappeared before he could even react. The attacker was another truck driver.

title: On Camera, ATM Dragged Out With Thar, Left Empty On Road Hours Later
content: Locals say there have been several incidents of theft and robbery in the region over the past few months, and residents have demanded increased police patrolling and stronger security measures.

title: China Evacuates Over 1 Million As Typhoon Bavi Nears
content: Earlier, at least 17 people were killed in the southern Philippines, mostly due to landslides that were set off by seasonal monsoon rains that Bavi intensified before the typhoon blew away toward Taiwan, Philippine officials said Saturday.
